topStories.description: The fate of an Iranian woman caught in a US-Israeli bomb attack is unknown, after video emerged of her filming it.

topStories.title: Elon Musk offers to pay salaries of unpaid TSA staff due to partial government shutdown
topStories.description: Amid the partial US government shutdown, Elon Musk proposed covering TSA personnel salaries. Essential TSA workers are struggling without pay, leading to staff shortages and long wait times at airports. Reports indicate some employees are seeking extra jobs or assistance.

topStories.title: Unease in Japan after Trump cites Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war

topStories.title: US & its allies' ‘attempts to silence voice’: Iranian-linked hacking group as its website back after FBI seized domains
topStories.description: Following a cyberattack on Stryker, the Iranian-linked Handala Hack Team's website went back online after the FBI seized its domains. The group claimed the seizures were attempts to suppress their voice, while Stryker is working to restore its systems and confirmed product safety.

topStories.title: Amid Hormuz Crisis, PM Speaks To Iran President, Stresses Open Shipping Lanes

topStories.title: Duggar couple arrested: What are the new charges against Joseph Duggar and Kendra Duggar?
topStories.description: Former reality star Joseph Duggar will be transferred to Florida to face child molestation charges after waiving his extradition hearing. He faces serious allegations, including false imprisonment, mirroring past legal troubles of his brother, Josh Duggar, who was convicted of child sexual abuse.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the day by Leo Tolstoy: ‘The two most powerful warriors are patience and...'
dailyNews.description: Leo Tolstoy's inspiring words serves a reminder that the two most powerful warriors are patience and time. While patience keeps us grounded in reality, time helps us in moving forward. The quote is extremely relevant in today's time, when everyone wants results instantly.

dailyNews.title: Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility
dailyNews.description: No leakage of radioactive materials reported in the area in central Iran, Tehran's atomic energy organisation says.

dailyNews.title: West Asia: US, Israeli strikes target Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, IAEA says no increase in off-site radiation levels
dailyNews.description: The US and Israel launched an attack on Saturday (local time), targeting Iran's Natanz uranium-enrichment facility. However, no radioactive leaks occurred, Reuters reported, citing Tasnim news agency.

dailyNews.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase
dailyNews.description: Jury finds that two tweets posted in May 2022 by Musk contained false statements.

dailyNews.title: PM Modi conveys Eid and Nowruz greetings to Iran President, stresses on open shipping lanes amid West Asia conflict
dailyNews.description: PM Modi spoke with Iranian President Dr. Masoud Pezeshkian, extending Eid and Nowruz greetings while emphasizing hope for peace and stability in West Asia. He condemned attacks on infrastructure, warning they disrupt global supply chains and stressed the need to secure shipping routes.

dailyNews.title: Maher was amused by Trump’s Pearl Harbor joke to Japanese leader, says ‘if Shane Gillis said it, you’d laugh’
dailyNews.description: Bill Maher defended Trump's Pearl Harbor joke to Japan's PM Takaichi, saying it wasn't appropriate but was funny, while criticizing the president's handling of Iran conflict.

dailyNews.title: Denise Richards debuts ‘shocking’ facelift transformation in before-and-after photos
dailyNews.description: Denise Richards reveals details of her facelift, including her daughters' pushback, divorce recovery and placing full trust in Dr. Ben Talei.

dailyNews.title: 'Love Is Blind’ stars call out ‘superficial’ big-city dating, say Midwest is the real ‘hidden gem’
dailyNews.description: "Love Is Blind" couple Vic St. John and Christine Hamilton reveal why Midwest dating differs from big cities, sharing insights on modern romance challenges.

dailyNews.description: Vanderbilt chancellor warns elite universities risk failure by prioritizing political activism over education mission amid Trump administration scrutiny.

dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump warns he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to arrest illegal immigrants if Democrats refuse to meet his demands to end the government shutdown.

dailyNews.description: As they sought to respond to charges of antisemitism and harassment on campus, administrators and trustees made promises. Many have been kept, but not all.

dailyNews.description: Sandro Castro has drawn scrutiny for his Instagram posts in which he flaunts a life of luxury while using satire to point out the very deterioration his family’s leadership helped create.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Hiding the Truth About the War in Iran
dailyNews.description: Whatever short-term gain the president thinks he is getting by lying about the war in Iran is far exceeded by the cost, for him, the country and the world.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to use ICE for airport security amid worsening delays
dailyNews.description: Wait times aren't expected to improve until government funding is restored and TSA officers receive paychecks.

dailyNews.title: Trump signals possible "winding down" in Iran as Marines head to Middle East
dailyNews.description: President Trump said Friday on social media that the U.S. is considering "winding down" its military efforts against Iran. Meanwhile, more than 2,000 Marines and more warships are headed to the region.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs deploying ground troops
dailyNews.description: President Trump is weighing whether to send possibly thousands of U.S. troops into Iran as he looks for a way to achieve some of his key goals and end the war, according to the two current U.S. officials, two former U.S. officials and another person familiar with the discussions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

dailyNews.title: Intelligence chiefs' testimony is out of sync with Trump's talking points
dailyNews.description: For the first time since the U.S. launched a war on Iran, this week top intelligence officials spoke publicly about the conflict.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Trump says he is considering 'winding down' Iran military efforts
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israel strikes.
